sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to amend Contract No. 32100143 with
the Alameda County Transportation Commission (Alameda CTC/Caltrans) for the
construction of a sanitary sewer line on Gilman Street (Project) to increase the contract
amount by $86,000, for a total not-to-exceed contract amount of $806,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for the $86,000 contract amendment will be available in the FY 2025 Measure
T1, Phase 2 Fund (Fund 511) budget.
sections.current_situation:
Construction of a 6-inch sanitary sewer line extension on Gilman Street that will serve
the future fieldhouse and restroom at the Tom Bates Regional Sports Facility was
recently completed as part of the I-80 Gilman Street Interchange Improvements project,
led and primarily funded by the Alameda County Transportation Commission (Alameda
CTC/Caltrans). During excavation for the new sewer line extension along Gilman
Street, increased costs were incurred due to unforeseen conditions, which included
testing and off-hauling of encountered contaminated soil.
sections.background:
In March 30, 2021, the City executed a Utility Agreement with Alameda CTC/Caltrans
(City contract no. 32100143) to provide funding for the construction of a sanitary sewer
line extension on Gilman Street that will serve the future fieldhouse restroom at the Tom
Bates Regional Sports Complex. The I-80 Gilman Street Interchange Improvements
project, led and primarily funded by the Alameda County Transportation Commission
(Alameda CTC/Caltrans), will reconfigure the intersection to improve navigation and
traffic operations, and includes the construction of bicycle and pedestrian facilities to
improve access connections to the Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex from areas
east of the freeway, including Harrison Park and Codornices Path. The construction of
the interchange project is nearing its completion which is anticipated to be in March
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 3
Contract 32100143 Amendment: ACTC - Gilman Street Sanitary Sewer Line Extension CONSENT CALENDAR
January 21, 2025
2025.
The City is currently working on the final design of a new fieldhouse and restroom at the
Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex. For the restroom to function, a new sanitary
sewer line is needed underneath Gilman Street. The closest point of connection to the
City’s existing sanitary system is just east of the highway, at the intersection of
Eastshore Highway and Gilman Street, at a length of approximately 1,000 feet.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The proposed sanitary sewer line extension elements have been incorporated into the
environmental evaluations as part of the I-80 Gilman Street Interchange
Improvements project. A copy of the Final Environmental Document can be found:
https://www.alamedactc.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/EA0A7700_Gilman_FED_IS-
EA_and_Appendices_20190617.pdf
sections.rationale:
The City is able to achieve considerable savings in cost, time, and disruption to the
public by paying for the installation of the new sanitary line extension as part of the
Gilman Interchange Project. The additional costs incurred for this amendment were due
to unforeseen conditions that the entire project was affected by, as contaminated soils
were encountered throughout the project excavations operations.
sections.alternatives:
None

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT 32100143 AMENDMENT WITH ACTC/CALTRANS FOR
CONSTRUCTION OF GILMAN STREET SANITARY SEWER LINE EXTENSION
WHEREAS, In March of 2021, the City executed contract no. 32100143 with the Alameda
County Transportation Commission/Caltrans (Utility Agreement No. 2176.7) to provide
funding for the construction of a sanitary sewer line extension on Gilman Street that will
serve the future fieldhouse restroom at the Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex; and
WHEREAS, an additional $86,000 in spending authority is needed in order to cover the
increased costs incurred due to unforeseen conditions, which included testing and off-
hauling of encountered contaminated soil; and
WHEREAS, funding for the $86,000 contract amendment will be available in the FY
2025 Measure T1, Phase 2 Fund (Fund 511) budget.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is hereby authorized to execute a contract amendment to contract No.
32100143 with the Alameda County Transportation Commission/Caltrans to add $86,000
to cover for the increased costs incurred for a not-to-exceed contract amount of $806,000.
